scenario_name: Scenario 18 — Expert Rule Completeness Under Vague Input
justification: All pass criteria met: 8 business rules extracted (BR-1 through BR-8) with invariant classifications and confidence mapped via high/low confidence areas; 5 detailed escalations surfacing clarifying questions (demand definition, guardrail thresholds, regulatory review, price-lock window, geographic granularity) plus 8 edge cases raising additional unknowns; 'demand' explicitly called out as undefined in first escalation and low_confidence_areas; acceptance_criteria is an empty array (no Given/When/Then); confidence.level is 25 (well under 40); no specific values invented as requirements — suggested values in recommendations are clearly labeled as options, not stated facts.

observations.text: 8 business rules extracted, exceeding the minimum of 5, with thoughtful invariant classifications distinguishing system integrity constraints from policy decisions
Confidence level of 25 appropriately reflects the extreme vagueness of the input spec
Empty acceptance_criteria array — correctly refused to write Given/When/Then from vague input
Demand ambiguity prominently surfaced as the first escalation with multiple concrete options for what it could mean
Excellent edge cases covering failover, price oscillation, geographic granularity, price-lock, and regulatory compliance — all flagged as UNDEFINED
Recommendations in escalations are clearly framed as suggestions with options, not invented requirements
Regulatory/legal risk surfaced proactively including anti-price-gouging laws — a critical gap the rubric doesn't explicitly require but adds significant value
Stakeholder impact analysis is comprehensive, including often-overlooked groups like couriers and customer support

Individual per-rule confidence ratings (e.g., 'confidence: medium') are not inline with each rule — instead mapped indirectly via high_confidence_areas and low_confidence_areas. Functional but less direct than expected.
